title: Lagrangian cobordisms, enriched knot diagrams, and algebraic invariants
speaker: Ipsita Datta
abstract: We introduce new invariants to the existence of Lagrangian cobordisms in R^4. These are obtained by studying holomorphic disks with corners on Lagrangian tangles, which are Lagrangian cobordisms with flat, immersed boundaries.   We develop appropriate sign conventions and results to characterize boundary points of 1-dimensional moduli spaces with boundaries on Lagrangian tangles. We then use these to define (SFT-like) algebraic structures that recover the previously described obstructions.

proof_stages.label: Enriched knot diagrams as slice data
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by encoding a generic knot projection by a planar immersed curve, crossing signs, and the areas of complementary regions, with equivalence given by area-preserving/Hamiltonian isotopy of the plane.

proof_stages.label: Undercuts relation from relatively exact Lagrangian cobordisms
proof_stages.summary: An enriched diagram is declared to undercut another when they arise as the lower and upper transverse slices of a relatively exact Lagrangian in standard R4, and on exact diagrams this relation gives a strict partial order.

proof_stages.label: Big/little disk obstruction theorem
proof_stages.summary: For proposed undercutting pairs the speaker defines big disks on one boundary and little disks relative to them on the other, then states that existence of the Lagrangian forces every big disk to dominate a corresponding little disk in area.

proof_stages.label: Slice algebra and disk-counting differential
proof_stages.summary: In the exact case each enriched slice is assigned a free associative algebra with upper/lower generators, a hat module action, floor signs, and an element h counting holomorphic disks, producing a differential whose homology recovers Legendrian contact homology when the slice is Legendrian.

proof_stages.label: Lagrangian tangles, tangle algebra, and the f-relations
proof_stages.summary: A Lagrangian cobordism is flattened by small Lagrangian collars to an immersed Lagrangian tangle, whose interior holomorphic disks define an element f in a larger hat algebra and hence a system of relations L_f used to restrict algebra elements.

proof_stages.label: Moduli-boundary identity recovering the obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The identity that the horizontal disk count h restricted by L_f vanishes is interpreted as the boundary of one-dimensional holomorphic-disk moduli spaces, where unbroken horizontal disks are the big disks and broken configurations supply the required little disks and area comparison.

proof_stages.label: Tangle homology and action-decreasing cobordism maps
proof_stages.summary: Using h followed by restriction to L_f gives a differential on the tangle algebra and inclusions followed by the same restriction give chain maps from both boundary slice complexes to the tangle complex, with both maps action-decreasing and reducing to identities in suitable Legendrian boundary cases.

examples.name: Legendrian-boundary identity cases
examples.note: Special cases where one boundary is Legendrian for an appropriate contact form illustrate why one of the two maps j-plus or j-minus becomes the identity, a point later clarified in Q&A.
